channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A key U.S. spy tool is set to lapse on Friday — now what?
item.description: The government says more than 60% of the president's daily intelligence briefing relies on information collected under a tool known as FISA Section 702. But Congress has struggled to renew it.
item.content:encoded:
The government says more than 60% of the president's daily intelligence briefing relies on information collected under a tool known as FISA Section 702. But Congress has struggled to renew it.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: It's SpaceX's first day on the stock market
item.description: The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.
item.content:encoded:
The initial public offering from the rocket and AI company raised some $75 billion, making the company one of the biggest in the world — and likely making Elon Musk a trillionaire.

(Image credit: RONALDO SCHEMIDT)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Canada is ready to become a soccer nation as it hosts World Cup opener against Bosnia-Herzegovina
item.description: The World Cup is drawing attention to soccer's growing popularity in Canada. Soccer has surpassed hockey and all other sports in youth participation, according to a recent report by Jumpstart, a Canadian charity. Canada coach Jesse Marsch said Thursday he has "felt a real momentum behind this team and behind this moment."
item.content:encoded:
The World Cup is drawing attention to soccer's growing popularity in Canada. Soccer has surpassed hockey and all other sports in youth participation, according to a recent report by Jumpstart, a Canadian charity. Canada coach Jesse Marsch said Thursday he has "felt a real momentum behind this team and behind this moment."

(Image credit: Stephanie Scarbrough)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pope Leo defends migrants at 'dock of shame' in Spain
item.description: Pope Leo XIV visited the Canary Islands on Thursday, where he issued a forceful defense of migrants.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV visited the Canary Islands on Thursday, where he issued a forceful defense of migrants.

(Image credit: Alessandra Tarantino)
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Trump cancels further Iran strikes. And, U.S. men's soccer takes on Paraguay
item.description: Trump canceled new Iran strikes and signaled that a peace deal could come soon, but Iran says it hasn't been finalized. And, the U.S. men's soccer team plays its first 2026 World Cup match today.
item.content:encoded:
Trump canceled new Iran strikes and signaled that a peace deal could come soon, but Iran says it hasn't been finalized. And, the U.S. men's soccer team plays its first 2026 World Cup match today.

(Image credit: Amirhosein Khorgooi)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Which billionaire said they learned a 'significant lesson' this week? The quiz knows
item.description: This week, Knicks fans had a big win after a big loss; fans of inflation were delighted and World Cup fans went broke. How will quiz fans fare?
item.content:encoded: This week, Knicks fans had a big win after a big loss; fans of inflation were delighted and World Cup fans went broke. How will quiz fans fare?
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: She waited decades for Scotland to make the World Cup. At 93, she'll cheer in person
item.description: Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.
item.content:encoded:
Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.

(Image credit: Lauren Frayer)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Door shuts on some immigrant entrepreneurs as U.S. restricts small-business loans
item.description: For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.
item.content:encoded:
For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Sayuri Tsuchitani)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Your cheat sheet to the 26 players on the U.S. World Cup team
item.description: The U.S. is opening its 2026 World Cup on Friday evening against Paraguay. For the 26 Americans on the team, this match is the culmination of years of hard work and training.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is opening its 2026 World Cup on Friday evening against Paraguay. For the 26 Americans on the team, this match is the culmination of years of hard work and training.

(Image credit: Nicky Quamina-Woo for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Trump's DOJ can't get names and medical files of trans youth in California, for now
item.description: Trump's Department of Justice is seeking patient files that include the names of young people who have been treated in transgender clinics, as well as hospital staff who have provided care.
item.content:encoded:
Trump's Department of Justice is seeking patient files that include the names of young people who have been treated in transgender clinics, as well as hospital staff who have provided care.

(Image credit: Heather Khalifa)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin
